title: madia elegans subsp. wheeleri
intro: Madia elegans subsp. wheeleri, also known as Wheeler's tarweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family, native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and fight off infections. The health benefits of Wheeler's tarweed include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
